mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contractions, triggering immediate equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.25 percentage points in 30 calendar days
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands while housing starts contract simultaneously, it indicates a rotation from construction-driven demand to manufacturing-driven demand that historically supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for leveraged corporations, compressing earnings growth expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.50 percentage points in a single policy cycle
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral during 60-day windows following rate hikes exceeding 50 basis points, or the forward return distribution shows no significant shift from historical medians.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real personal consumption expenditures reflects underlying consumer spending strength that directly boosts corporate top-line growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: The monthly change in RSAFS exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly from zero in a two-tailed t-test.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in the industrial production index below its 12-month moving average signals supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that erode corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The index level fails to predict subsequent equity declines, or forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO trades below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and accelerating consumer price inflation indicates wage-price spiral dynamics that force prolonged restrictive monetary policy, ultimately suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not produce a statistically significant negative skew in quarterly forward returns, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-pandemic normalization phase, a sharp deceleration in real savings accumulation forces households to draw down balances to maintain consumption, temporarily sustaining corporate revenues and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The deceleration in real savings correlates with negative equity returns in more than 50% of observations, or the forward return spread between high-deceleration and low-deceleration months is statistically insignificant.
